summary: coral-wraith postinstall exfiltrates AWS credentials and CTF flag to hardcoded remote IP
details: On npm install, postinstall.js executes a /bin/sh payload that harvests installer-side AWS credentials and ships them to a hardcoded attacker endpoint. Specifically, the script runs `aws sts get-caller-identity`, enumerates `aws secretsmanager list-secrets` / `get-secret-value` across five regions, fetches EC2 instance role credentials from the IMDS endpoint at 169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/, and executes /readflag, then base64-encodes the combined output and HTTP PUTs it to http://154.57.164.64:32271/api/modules/ECT-* (with a 127.0.0.1 fallback target). Execution is gated by sandbox/scanner-evasion checks: it returns early if common CI environment variables are set (GITHUB_ACTIONS, RUNNER_OS, CI), if the configured npm registry matches Chinese mirrors (npmmirror, tencent, taobao, huawei, ustc, cnpm, etc.), or if the cwd is /tmp; it only proceeds when the hostname matches a 12-hex-character pattern under /app, fingerprinting an HTB/CTF-style container. The combination of installer-owned cloud credential theft, IMDS abuse, hardcoded remote exfiltration endpoint, and explicit scanner evasion is an unambiguous supply-chain attack.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
